summary: I was born in Knoxville, Tennessee, on May 15th, 1877. My parents were George and a in aie a right, though I don't recall much about my early years there. I haven't got any estimation how long I lived in Tennessee before moving on. Knoxville is where my story begins, but most of my life and work happened elsewhere.
cues: parents George and a in aie a right

summary: After Tennessee, I lived in Forsyth, Monroe County, Georgia, where I picked cotton. I married my first wife, Isabella, there, though I can't recall the exact year. Monroe County was part of my early adult life before I moved to Florida. It was a time of hard work and family beginnings for me.

I've been living near Lakeland, Florida, for about thirty-eight years now. I often go to town there to sell vegetables and trade, though sometimes folks trick me, like when my goats ran off in the rain and hid under a house in Lakeland. I also had some trouble with a man there over a goat sale, which led to some hard feelings. Lakeland is the nearest town where I do my trading and selling.

I live in Polk County, Florida, but I don't participate in voting because I was told that's white folks' business, not mine. This county is where my home and farm are, where I trap animals and grow vegetables. Life here is simple, without modern conveniences like electricity or radio, but I manage with what I have. Polk County is where I raise my family and make my living.
